text: The surgeon carefully dissects the thymus gland located in the chest cavity, using precise techniques to separate it from surrounding tissues.
The surgeon uses a minimally invasive approach with three small incisions in the chest to access the thymus, avoiding a traditional open chest procedure.
The surgeon uses ultrasonic shears through the thoracic ports to dissect tissue within the chest cavity, focusing on the removal of the thymus and associated thymoma.
The surgeon uses ultrasonic shears to cut tissue, utilizing ultrasound energy to avoid burning the tissue during the thymectomy procedure.
The surgeon gently dissects the tumor and thymus, carefully shelling out the tumor from surrounding tissue using precise movements.
The heart is visibly pumping beneath the tumor, which is located in the thymus region. The thymoma is being removed along with the thymus tissue.
The surgeon gently dissects the thymus glandular structure, carefully navigating around a large vein visible in the surgical field to prevent bleeding from smaller veins.
